codeReview.summary: Clean, correct implementation. The commit adds a single `/*.png` gitignore rule that is root-anchored (only blocks PNGs in the repo root, not subdirectories). The worker also deleted 19 untracked validation PNG files from the root. Current state confirms no PNGs remain in the root directory while `docs/screenshot.png` and `.factory/validation/` PNGs are unaffected. The gitignore comment clearly explains the scoping. All three expected behaviors from the feature spec are met: no stale PNGs in root, gitignore updated, subdirectory PNGs preserved.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature description mentions specific PNG filenames to remove but the worker found 19 files (more than the 7 listed). The worker correctly interpreted the instruction 'and any other *.png files in the repo root that are validation artifacts' and removed all of them. No convention gap — the feature description was sufficiently clear.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff says '19 stale PNG screenshot files'; feature description lists 7 by name plus 'any other *.png files in the repo root that are validation artifacts'.

summary: Pass. The worker correctly removed all stale validation PNG files from the repo root and added a root-scoped `/*.png` gitignore rule. The implementation is minimal, correct, and well-scoped. PNGs in subdirectories (docs/, .factory/validation/) are unaffected. All validators (typecheck, lint, 84 tests) pass.
